name: NM Control: Controlling during Run-Time
title: NM Control: Controlling during Run-Time
intro: With the NM Control you can control the OSEK NM DLL (oseknm01.dll) or the AutoSAR NM DLLs (AsrNM30.dll, AsrNM33.dll) on a simulated CAN bus in CANoe.
The NM control lists all nodes that have a corresponding DLL assigned for the node in the Simulation Setup.

paragraphs: ID
Node address relevant for the NM.
RM
With this button you activate the Repeat Message Mode (only possible with own Bus Request).
IL
A green LED indicates that the Interaction Layer was activated.
A gray LED indicates that the Interaction Layer was not activated.
Act

This LED can be used as an operator control element. Here you can activate/deactivate the Application Activity in the NM with a click. This normally prevents the bus from being released even if an internal request is not present (see first column).
A yellow LED indicates that the Application Activity was activated in the NM.
NM
This LED can be used as an operator control element. Here you can stop the simulation of the NM DLL (CtrlSimulationOff) or start it (CtrlSimulationOn) with a click.
A red LED indicates that the simulation of the NM DLL was stopped.
State
State of the respective state machine of the NM DLL.
* PN: Partial Networks
tables: In the first column you specify whether the bus will be requested by the NM (Bus Request) or not (Bus Release).
Name of the respective node from the Simulation Setup.
Node address relevant for the NM.
With this button you activate the Repeat Message Mode (only possible with own Bus Request).
A green LED indicates that the Interaction Layer was activated. A gray LED indicates that the Interaction Layer was not activated.

This LED can be used as an operator control element. Here you can activate/deactivate the Application Activity in the NM with a click. This normally prevents the bus from being released even if an internal request is not present (see first column). A yellow LED indicates that the Application Activity was activated in the NM.

This LED can be used as an operator control element. Here you can stop the simulation of the NM DLL (CtrlSimulationOff) or start it (CtrlSimulationOn) with a click. A red LED indicates that the simulation of the NM DLL was stopped.
State of the respective state machine of the NM DLL.

paragraphs: The buttons at the bottom edge of the panel initiate actions on all nodes.
[NM Request All]
[NM Release All]
[IL Request All]
[IL Release All]
For the AutoSAR NM with Partial Networks (AsrNM33.dll), the panel has a splitter on the right side.
With the splitter you open/close a Detail view with display and control elements for the node that you have selected on the left side under Nodes.
The Detail view displays all Partial Networks of the node with name and network ID.
On the far right it is indicated whether the node
The green LED indicates that the corresponding Partial Network is currently being requested for this node (internally or externally).
The control field indicates (or controls) whether or not the node requests this network with a Bus Request (control field on the left side).
Note
If the control fields and LEDs of a node are not visible, either the node is deactivated in the Simulation Setup or its NM state is passive.
You will find additional information about the behavior and special features/functions of the NM DLL and its states here:
